visualizerParams.title: Square Root Convergents
visualizerParams.description: Calculate how many of the first 1000 expansions of the square root of 2 have a numerator with more digits than the denominator.
visualizerParams.ghostCode:
def solution(n: int = 1000) -> int:
    """
    Returns the number of fractions in the first n expansions of sqrt(2)
    where the numerator has more digits than the denominator.
    """
    # sqrt(2) = 1 + 1/2, 1 + 1/(2 + 1/2), ...
    # Expansions: 3/2, 7/5, 17/12, 41/29, ...
    # Recurrence relation for n-th expansion n_i / d_i:
    # n_{i+1} = n_i + 2*d_i
    # d_{i+1} = n_i + d_i
    
    num, den = 3, 2
    count = 0
    
    for _ in range(n - 1): # First expansion (3/2) is already handled
        if len(str(num)) > len(str(den)):
            count += 1
        
        num, den = num + 2 * den, num + den
        
    # Check the last expansion
    if len(str(num)) > len(str(den)):
        count += 1
        
    return count


if __name__ == "__main__":
    print(f"Count: {solution(1000)}")

    """
    Returns the number of fractions in the first n expansions of sqrt(2)
    where the numerator has more digits than the denominator.
    """
    # sqrt(2) = 1 + 1/2, 1 + 1/(2 + 1/2), ...
    # Expansions: 3/2, 7/5, 17/12, 41/29, ...
    # Recurrence relation for n-th expansion n_i / d_i:
    # n_{i+1} = n_i + 2*d_i
    # d_{i+1} = n_i + d_i
    
    num, den = 3, 2
    count = 0
    
    for _ in range(n - 1): # First expansion (3/2) is already handled
        if len(str(num)) > len(str(den)):
            count += 1
        
        num, den = num + 2 * den, num + den
        
    # Check the last expansion
    if len(str(num)) > len(str(den)):
